info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Plan Balances
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

parameters.name: planBalancesUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Plan Balances resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Plan Balances. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Plan Balances collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Plan Balances to get the hash key.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

parameters.name: Effective-Of
parameters.description: This header accepts a string value. The string is a semi-colon separated list of <parameter-name>=<parameter-value>. It is used to perform effective date range operations. The accepted parameters are RangeMode, RangeSpan, RangeStartDate, RangeEndDate, RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence. The parameter values are always strings. The possible values for RangeMode are SET_LOGICAL_START, SET_LOGICAL_END, END_DATE, SET_EFFECTIVE_START, SET_EFFECTIVE_END, REPLACE_CORRECTION, REPLACE_UPDATE, RECONCILE_CORRECTION, CORRECTION, RECONCILE_UPDATE, UPDATE, ZAP and DELETE_CHANGES. The possible values for RangeSpan are PHYSICAL_ROW_END_DATE and LOGICAL_ROW_END_DATE. The values for RangeStartDate and RangeEndDate have to be a string representation of a date in yyyy-MM-dd format. The value for RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence must be strings such that when parsed they yield positive integers.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

planBalanceSummary.description: The planBalanceSummary resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides summary-level information on all transactions that impact the balance on the plan.

unvestedDetails.description: The unvestedBalanceDetails resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides the details of balance transactions that are unvested.

yearEndDetails.description: The yearEndDetails resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides the details for the plan year end transactions.

details.description: The details resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides details of each transaction type available for the absence plan.

planBalanceDetails.description: The planBalanceDetails resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides detailed information on all transactions that impact the balance on the plan.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • SysEffectiveDate; string
  • assignmentId; integer; Unique identifier of the worker's assignment to which the plan is enrolled.
  • assignmentName; string; Name of the worker's assignment to which the plan is enrolled.
  • assignmentNumber; string; Number of the worker's assignment to which the plan is enrolled.
  • balanceAsOfBalanceCalculationDate; number; Remaining plan balance amount for a person on the balance calculation date.
  • balanceCalculationDate; string; Date on which accrual is run to calculate the balance for the person.
  • ceilingAmount; number; Ceiling amount shown on the donation absence plan.
  • effectiveEndDate; string
  • effectiveStartDate; string
  • enrollPositiveBalanceFlag; boolean; Disburse checkbox enabled in absence plan.
  • enrollmentEndDate; string; Date on which the person is removed from plan when plan enrollment ends.
  • enrollmentStartDate; string; Date on which the person is enrolled to the plan.
  • formattedBalance; string; Remaining plan balance amount for a person on the balance calculation date, formatted according to user preference.
  • level; string
  • personId; integer; Unique identifier for the person.
  • personPlanEnrollmentId; integer; Unique identifier for a worker's plan enrollment.
  • planDisplayStatusFlag; boolean; Indicates whether the plan balance can be displayed to users, as defined in the absence type. If true, balance can be displayed to users. If false, balance can't be displayed to users.
  • planEnrollmentStatus; string; Status of plan enrollment as of current date, such as Active or Inactive.
  • planId; integer; Unique identifier of the plan.
  • planName; string; Name of the plan.
  • planPeriodEndDate; string; End date of the plan period.
  • planPeriodStartDate; string; Start date of the plan period.
  • planStatus; string; Status of plan, such as A for Active or V for Inactive.
  • planStatusMeaning; string; Meaning of plan status, such as Active, Inactive.
  • planType; string; Type of absence plan used to record an absence, such as Accrual, Donation.
  • planTypeCode; string; Code of the plan type.
  • planUnitOfMeasure; string; Unique identifier assigned to a unit of measure for absence plan, such as D or H.
  • recipientAliasName; string; Alias name of the donation recipient.
  • transferRule; string; Potential transfer of absence plan to be evaluated for transfer of plan balance after global transfer transaction.
  • unitOfMeasureMeaning; string; Meaning of plan unit of measure, such as Days or Hours.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds all the plan balances that match the primary key criteria specified.
    Finder Variables
    • personPlanEnrollmentId; integer; Unique identifier of a plan enrollment.
  • findByAssignmentIdPlanIdLevelDate Finds plan balances based on the specified attributes.
    Finder Variables
    • SysEffectiveDate; object;
    • assignmentId; integer; Unique Identifier of the worker's assignment.
    • balanceAsOfDate; string; Date for which balance is calculated.
    • includeFutureNegative; string; Plan balances that include negative value in the future.
    • level; string; Level of detail expected in the response. Possible values are balanceSummary, balanceDetail or all.
    • planId; integer; Unique identifier of the plan.
  • findByCurrentDate Finds plan balances based on the current date.
    Finder Variables
    • SysEffectiveDate; object;
    • assignmentId; integer; Unique Identifier for the worker's assignment.
    • personId; integer; Unique identifier for the person.
    • planId; integer; Unique identifier for the plan.
  • findByLastCalculationDate Finds the balance as of the last calculation date.
    Finder Variables
    • SysEffectiveDate; object;
    • assignmentId; integer; Unique Identifier of the worker's assignment.
    • personId; integer; Unique identifier for the person.
    • planId; integer; Unique identifier for the plan.
  • findByPersonIdPlanIdLevelDate Finds plan balances based on the specified attributes.
    Finder Variables
    • SysEffectiveDate; object;
    • balanceAsOfDate; string; Date for which balance is calculated.
    • level; string; Level of detail expected in the response. Possible values are balanceSummary, balanceDetail or all.
    • personId; integer; Unique identifier of the person.
    • planId; integer; Unique identifier of the plan.

parameters.name: effectiveDate
parameters.description: This query parameter is used to fetch resources which are effective dated as of the specified start date. The value of this query parameter is a string having a date in yyyy-MM-dd format

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

parameters.name: Effective-Of
parameters.description: This header accepts a string value. The string is a semi-colon separated list of <parameter-name>=<parameter-value>. It is used to perform effective date range operations. The accepted parameters are RangeMode, RangeSpan, RangeStartDate, RangeEndDate, RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence. The parameter values are always strings. The possible values for RangeMode are SET_LOGICAL_START, SET_LOGICAL_END, END_DATE, SET_EFFECTIVE_START, SET_EFFECTIVE_END, REPLACE_CORRECTION, REPLACE_UPDATE, RECONCILE_CORRECTION, CORRECTION, RECONCILE_UPDATE, UPDATE, ZAP and DELETE_CHANGES. The possible values for RangeSpan are PHYSICAL_ROW_END_DATE and LOGICAL_ROW_END_DATE. The values for RangeStartDate and RangeEndDate have to be a string representation of a date in yyyy-MM-dd format. The value for RangeStartSequence and RangeEndSequence must be strings such that when parsed they yield positive integers.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

assignmentId.title: Assignment ID
assignmentId.description: Unique identifier of the worker's assignment to which the plan is enrolled.

assignmentName.title: Assignment Name
assignmentName.description: Name of the worker's assignment to which the plan is enrolled.

assignmentNumber.title: Assignment Number
assignmentNumber.description: Number of the worker's assignment to which the plan is enrolled.

balanceAsOfBalanceCalculationDate.description: Remaining plan balance amount for a person on the balance calculation date.

balanceCalculationDate.description: Date on which accrual is run to calculate the balance for the person.

ceilingAmount.description: Ceiling amount shown on the donation absence plan.

details.title: Details
details.description: The details resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides details of each transaction type available for the absence plan.

enrollPositiveBalanceFlag.description: Disburse checkbox enabled in absence plan.

enrollmentEndDate.description: Date on which the person is removed from plan when plan enrollment ends.

enrollmentStartDate.description: Date on which the person is enrolled to the plan.

formattedBalance.description: Remaining plan balance amount for a person on the balance calculation date, formatted according to user preference.

formattedCeilingAmount.description: Format of the ceiling amount displayed on a donation plan.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

multiYearCarryOverFlag.description: Indicates whether the plan has multiyear carryover enabled. If true, multiple years of carryover will be displayed. If false, carryover will only show for one year.

personPlanEnrollmentId.description: Unique identifier for a worker's plan enrollment.

planBalanceDetails.title: Plan Balance Details
planBalanceDetails.description: The planBalanceDetails resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides detailed information on all transactions that impact the balance on the plan.

planBalanceSummary.title: Plan Balance Summaries
planBalanceSummary.description: The planBalanceSummary resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides summary-level information on all transactions that impact the balance on the plan.

planDisplayStatusFlag.description: Indicates whether the plan balance can be displayed to users, as defined in the absence type. If true, balance can be displayed to users. If false, balance can't be displayed to users.

planEnrollmentStatus.description: Status of plan enrollment as of current date, such as Active or Inactive.

planPeriodEndDate.description: End date of the plan period.

planPeriodStartDate.description: Start date of the plan period.

planStatus.description: Status of plan, such as A for Active or V for Inactive.

planStatusMeaning.description: Meaning of plan status, such as Active, Inactive.

planType.title: Meaning
planType.description: Type of absence plan used to record an absence, such as Accrual, Donation.

planUnitOfMeasure.description: Unique identifier assigned to a unit of measure for absence plan, such as D or H.

recipientAliasName.description: Alias name of the donation recipient.

transferRule.description: Potential transfer of absence plan to be evaluated for transfer of plan balance after global transfer transaction.

unitOfMeasureMeaning.title: Meaning
unitOfMeasureMeaning.description: Meaning of plan unit of measure, such as Days or Hours.

unvestedDetails.title: Unvested Balance Details
unvestedDetails.description: The unvestedBalanceDetails resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides the details of balance transactions that are unvested.

yearEndDetails.title: Year End Details
yearEndDetails.description: The yearEndDetails resource is a child of the planBalances resource and provides the details for the plan year end transactions.

additionalInformation.description: Display of additional information about the transaction.

bucketSequence.description: Displayed sequence of the transaction types for the plan.

carryoverExpiryDate.description: Date the carryover balance will expire.

expiryDate.description: Expiration date of the transaction or balance.

expiryDateOverReasonCode.description: Code of the expiration date override.

expiryDateOverReasonMeaning.description: Reason the expiration date was overridden on the transaction.

formattedRemainingBalance.description: Format of the remaining carryover balance.

formattedTransactionAmount.description: Format of the absence transaction amount.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

manualAdjustmentReasonCode.description: Code of the manual adjustment reason.

manualAdjustmentReasonMeaning.description: Reason for the manual adjustment transaction on the plan.

overrideExpiryDate.description: Override expiry date for a compensatory plan transaction.

planBalanceDetailsId.description: Unique identifier of the plan balance details.

planEndDate.description: End date of the absence plan term.

projectedStatusFlag.description: Indicates whether the transaction type balance is projected. If true, the balance is a projected balance. If false, the balance is the actual balance.

voidFlag.description: Indicates whether a transaction has been voided. If true, the transaction is no longer valid. If false, the transaction is still valid and not voided.
